content: I've been trying to cook more traditional Portuguese dishes since I moved here from the US three years ago. Last night I attempted bacalhau à brás for the first time.
That's one of the most classic Portuguese dishes! How did your bacalhau à brás turn out? The balance of salted cod, potatoes, and eggs can be tricky to get right.

It was actually pretty good! I think I used a bit too much olive oil, but my parents who are visiting from Florida really enjoyed it. They're staying with me for a month while I help them sort out some paperwork.
That's wonderful that you're cooking for them. How are they adjusting to Lisbon after living in Florida? The food scene here is quite different from what they're probably used to.

They love it, though my dad misses his American breakfast foods. I've been trying to find a middle ground - made him sourdough pancakes the other day using my starter. He said they reminded him of the ones my grandmother used to make.
Sourdough pancakes sound like the perfect compromise! Since you're running a startup, do you find cooking helps you unwind from the long hours of building your AI tools company?
Definitely. Between fundraising and product development, I'm at my computer way too much. Cooking forces me to step away and use my hands. I've even started 3D printing custom cookie cutters for fun.
3D printed cookie cutters? That's brilliant! What shapes have you made? I bet you could create some really intricate Portuguese-themed designs.

I started with simple geometric patterns, but now I'm working on azulejo tile designs. My mom's been doing origami with the kids I mentor, so we're planning a combined baking and paper-folding afternoon next weekend.
That sounds like such a creative combination! How do you balance everything - the startup, caring for your parents, mentoring, and all these hobbies? You must have incredible time management skills.
